footer.tagline: AITrans.Video makes the world's videos speak Chinese. A long-form AI translation and dubbing workbench with Chinese subtitles, Chinese voiceover, multi-format export, and sentence-by-sentence editing.
footer.copyright: © {year} AITrans.Video · Long-form video translation & dubbing workbench

comparison.eyebrow: How it differs from one-click tools
comparison.heading: One-click generators vs. a long-form translation & dubbing workbench
comparison.lead: This isn't a competitor review — it explains two product shapes. For quick short-video tests a one-click generator is fine; for ongoing long-form localization that needs review and fine polishing, the workbench model saves more time and money.

rows.oneClick: Better for short-video tests (seconds to a few minutes)
rows.workbench: Handles 1–3 hour long videos, tuned for interviews, courses, and podcasts

rows.oneClick: After generation you can essentially only re-run the whole clip — expensive to change
rows.workbench: Review translation and dubbing sentence by sentence in the workbench, regenerate single segments

rows.oneClick: Usually just a single finished clip
rows.workbench: Dubbed video / dubbed audio / subtitles / translated text / materials pack / Jianying draft

rows.oneClick: Changing one sentence means re-running the whole clip — minutes pile up fast
rows.workbench: Change one sentence and only pay for that one — fine-grained billing by duration

faq.heading: You might be wondering
faq.marqueeHint: Hover to pause auto-scroll · see the full Q&A on the
faq.supportNudge: Still have questions? Click the "Support" widget at the bottom right — AI first, then a human.

general.q: Why do you emphasize long videos?
general.a: A lot of knowledge content isn't a few-second short — it's a 30-minute, 1-hour, or even 3-hour interview, course, podcast, or talk. Our Pro plan supports a single video up to 180 minutes long, built exactly for translating and Chinese-dubbing this kind of long-form content.

Right now we focus on translating overseas English long videos into Chinese-dubbed versions — interviews, courses, podcasts, and product demos work most reliably. Other source languages (Japanese, Spanish, etc.) are under evaluation and not yet available. If you plan to upload non-English material, please check with support first.

We support importing your own or duly authorized content: local video file upload, YouTube links (for your own channel or videos you have rights to), and other video links. Before using, please confirm you hold the legal rights to the video — see the Terms of Service for details.

general.q: How is this different from Rask, HeyGen, or ElevenLabs?
general.a: Rask, HeyGen, and ElevenLabs are all excellent global platforms, some very strong on digital avatars or voice cloning. AITrans.Video's focus is different — we care more about what Chinese creators actually need when handling overseas English long videos: longer video durations, a lower barrier to entry, multiple export formats, and the ability to edit sentence by sentence and regenerate single segments after generation.

general.q: What if I'm not happy with the AI translation?
general.a: What the AI generates is a first draft, not the final version. In the workbench you can review the translation, subtitles, and dubbing sentence by sentence, edit any sentence you're not happy with, and regenerate just that segment.

general.q: Do I have to regenerate the whole video to change one sentence?
general.a: No. AITrans.Video supports incremental regeneration: when you change just one sentence, the system reprocesses only that segment instead of re-synthesizing the whole video, saving both time and cost.

general.q: What's the difference between Studio mode and Express mode?
general.a: Express mode runs the whole pipeline automatically — fast and low-cost, good for validating results first or producing in batches. Studio mode lets you review the translation sentence by sentence in the workbench and choose a more fitting Chinese dubbing voice, with more stable quality, better suited for content you'll publish or deliver to clients. With proper legal authorization, Studio also supports voice cloning.

Once a job is done you can download: the Chinese-dubbed video, dubbed audio, Chinese subtitles (including English / bilingual subtitles), translated text, the raw materials pack, and a Jianying draft project — open the finished subtitle, dubbing, and material tracks directly in Jianying to keep fine-editing, without laying out the timeline from scratch.

pricingExtra.q: What happens after the trial ends?
pricingExtra.a: When the trial ends there's no automatic charge. You can keep using the free allowance of the Free plan, or upgrade to Plus or Pro on your own.
By the actual duration of the source video. After processing completes, that duration counts toward your current allowance; failed or canceled jobs are not billed.

pricingExtra.q: What exactly does "no charge on failure" mean?
pricingExtra.a: If a job fails during system processing and produces no downloadable result, the corresponding processing allowance is not deducted; jobs you cancel before completion are also not billed. Note that "not satisfied" doesn't count here — results that are completed and delivered are billed normally by duration, but you can edit sentence by sentence and regenerate single segments in the workbench to avoid paying to re-run the whole clip.

painPoints.heading: With overseas long videos, the real work is the last 80%
painPoints.lead: Built for English source, Chinese output. Getting an AI first pass is easy; the hard part is editing it until it's truly ready to publish.
painPoints.closing: AITrans.Video isn't just "one-click generation" — it puts long-video translation, AI dubbing, multi-format delivery, and line-by-line editing into one workbench.

points.title: Videos run long
points.body: Many tools are built for testing the waters with clips of a few seconds to a few minutes. Once you hit a 30-minute, 1-hour, or even 3-hour interview, course, or podcast, both processing cost and wait time climb fast.

points.title: Results are hard to fix
points.body: An AI first pass always has issues with names, terminology, sentence breaks, and tone. Fixing one line often means re-running the whole video — wasting both time and money.

points.title: Too few deliverables
points.body: A single finished cut isn't enough. Creators also need Chinese subtitles, dubbed audio, and the raw materials pack to keep editing, republish, or send to clients for review.

points.title: Cost is hard to control
points.body: Overseas tools work well, but minutes, revisions, and added segments pile up the bill fast. When you ship often, a month's invoice routinely runs over budget.

productProof.heading: Not a one-shot generator, but a workbench you can review, edit, and download from
productProof.lead: AITrans.Video runs the full video translation and dubbing flow on-site: create a task, track progress, review line by line, edit and regenerate, then download the dubbed video, dubbed audio, subtitles, and materials pack. The screenshots below are from the product UI running right now.
productProof.card1Alt: New translation task page: YouTube link / video upload toggle + Express / Studio dual-plan choice + create-task button
productProof.card2Alt: Project results list page: each task card shows the title, expiry time, download buttons for dubbed video / audio / materials pack, and an edit entry
productProof.card3Title: Review the translation line by line on the timeline
productProof.card3Alt: Studio translation review screen: nine-step progress bar + speaker confirmation + line-by-line Chinese–English editing
productProof.card4Alt: Studio voice selection screen: MiniMax / CosyVoice / Doubao three-engine switch + preview / check against original / voice clone
taskFlow: Supports both YouTube links and video uploads — fit for real production inputs.
Express suits automated flows; Studio suits human review and fine-tuning.
Choose the transcription option, speaker count, and processing mode — not a template placeholder form.
resultFlow: After a task finishes, the project list, expiry time, processing status, and history are all kept.
Every project lets you download the dubbed video, dubbed audio, and materials pack.
A results page like this is direct proof that the site delivers a real digital service.

workflowShowcase.heading: From English video to finished Chinese cut, in four steps
workflowShowcase.lead: Every step can be paused, checked, and edited. AI produces the first pass; you decide which segments need another round.

steps.title: Import the video
steps.body: Upload a local video, or paste a link you have the right to process. Great for interviews, courses, podcasts, talks, and educational long videos.

steps.title: Generate the first pass
steps.body: The system automatically handles speech recognition, translation, subtitle generation, and Chinese dubbing. Get a previewable version quickly.

steps.title: Refine in the workbench
steps.body: Check the translation, subtitles, and dubbing line by line. Unhappy with a name, term, tone, or sentence break? Edit it and regenerate just that segment.

steps.title: Download the results
steps.body: Download the Chinese dubbed video, audio, subtitles, and materials pack — or export a Jianying draft and keep fine-cutting in Jianying without building the timeline from scratch.

featuredDemos.heading: One real dub says more than ten screenshots
featuredDemos.lead: Just listen — whether the translation sounds human, the dubbing sounds natural, and the pacing matches the original.
